theme: Metal that remembers differently when bent hard
title: Die-Bending Springback — recovering a thickness-shifted regime boundary
strategies: constant baseline 1.2 (ignores r,t entirely) -> reproduces the checker baseline ~0.1

greedy: assume a SINGLE textbook power law S = a * (r/t)^b, fit a,b by log-log least squares on the training (thin-sheet) rows; near-perfect on the elastic-dominated training cloud, but a lone power law in the ratio r/t cannot express that the SAME r/t value is elastic-dominated for thin sheets and plastic-saturated for thick ones -> systematically wrong on the held-out thick-sheet split where the dominant mechanism has flipped

strong: notice that no single curve in r/t explains the full training cloud; grid-search a candidate boundary line xc(t) = A - Bc*t, split training rows by x=r/t vs xc(t) at each candidate, fit an elastic branch (through-origin linear regression in x) below it and a plastic branch (linear regression in x^(1/3)) above it, and keep the (A,Bc) that minimises total training error; emit the branch-selecting expression via step(...) gating -- this recovers the regime boundary's t-dependence itself, so it also correctly reclassifies the held-out thick-sheet rows
headroom above strong: robust (non-quadratic) branch fits and a finer boundary search to push held-out MSE further toward the sensor-noise floor without hitting the ceiling
